categories.name: type
docs: Type nodes — the building blocks of every value shape.
Wraps a number type to provide additional context such as decimal places and a unit.
Useful for amounts representing financial values.

attributes.name: decimals
docs: The number of decimal places the wrapped integer carries.
For example, an integer value of 12345 with 2 decimal places represents 123.45.

attributes.name: unit
docs: The unit of the amount — e.g. "USD" or "%".

attributes.name: number
docs: The number type the amount wraps.
A homogeneous list of items. The item type is defined by `item`; the length is determined by the `count` strategy.

attributes.name: item
docs: The type of each item in the array.

attributes.name: count
docs: The strategy used to determine the number of items.
A boolean serialised as a numeric value. The wrapped number type determines the byte width.

attributes.name: size
docs: The numeric type used to serialise the boolean.
A raw sequence of bytes. Typically used inside a fixed-size, size-prefixed, or sentinel-terminated wrapper.
A timestamp encoded as a number, typically seconds since the Unix epoch. The wrapped number type determines the byte width.

attributes.name: number
docs: The numeric type used to serialise the timestamp.
A unit-style variant of an enum that carries no payload.

attributes.name: name
docs: The name of the variant.

attributes.name: discriminator
docs: Explicit discriminator value. When omitted, the discriminator is inferred from the variant position.

attributes.name: display
docs: Display metadata describing how the variant is presented.
A variant of an enum that carries a struct payload (named fields).

attributes.name: name
docs: The name of the variant.

attributes.name: discriminator
docs: Explicit discriminator value. When omitted, the discriminator is inferred from the variant position.

attributes.name: struct
docs: The struct of named fields carried by the variant.

attributes.name: display
docs: Display metadata describing how the variant is presented.
A variant of an enum that carries a tuple payload (positional fields).

attributes.name: name
docs: The name of the variant.

attributes.name: discriminator
docs: Explicit discriminator value. When omitted, the discriminator is inferred from the variant position.

attributes.name: tuple
docs: The tuple of positional fields carried by the variant.

attributes.name: display
docs: Display metadata describing how the variant is presented.
A tagged union: a numeric discriminator followed by one of several variant payloads.

attributes.name: variants
docs: The variants of the enum, in declaration order.

attributes.name: size
docs: The numeric type used to serialise the discriminator.
Wraps another type and asserts a fixed total byte size. Padding or truncation is applied as needed.

attributes.name: size
docs: The total byte size the wrapped type must occupy.

attributes.name: type
docs: The wrapped type whose serialisation is constrained.
Prefixes another type with a list of constant values that are written and read but not surfaced as fields to consumers.

attributes.name: type
docs: The wrapped type whose serialisation is preceded by the hidden prefix.

attributes.name: prefix
docs: The constant values written before the wrapped type, in order.
Suffixes another type with a list of constant values that are written and read but not surfaced as fields to consumers.

attributes.name: type
docs: The wrapped type whose serialisation is followed by the hidden suffix.

attributes.name: suffix
docs: The constant values written after the wrapped type, in order.
A keyed map.
The key and value types are described by their respective type nodes; the entry count is determined by a count strategy.

attributes.name: key
docs: The type of each entry key.

attributes.name: value
docs: The type of each entry value.

attributes.name: count
docs: The strategy used to determine the number of entries.
A numeric type with a fixed wire format and byte order.

attributes.name: format
docs: The wire format used to serialise the number.

attributes.name: endian
docs: The byte order used to serialise the number.

attributes.name: display
docs: Display metadata describing how the number is presented.
A value that may be present or absent (Some/None), with an explicit numeric prefix indicating presence.

attributes.name: fixed
docs: When `true`, the absent variant still occupies the byte size of the present variant (zero-padded). Defaults to `false`.

attributes.name: item
docs: The type carried by the option when present.

attributes.name: prefix
docs: The numeric type used as the presence flag.
After serialising the wrapped type, advance the cursor by `offset` bytes interpreted via the chosen strategy.

attributes.name: offset
docs: The signed byte offset to apply after the wrapped type runs.

attributes.name: strategy
docs: How the `offset` value is interpreted.

attributes.name: type
docs: The wrapped type whose serialisation is followed by the offset.
Before serialising the wrapped type, advance the cursor by `offset` bytes interpreted via the chosen strategy.

attributes.name: offset
docs: The signed byte offset to apply before the wrapped type runs.

attributes.name: strategy
docs: How the `offset` value is interpreted.

attributes.name: type
docs: The wrapped type whose serialisation is preceded by the offset.
A 32-byte Solana public key.
A value that may be present or absent. Presence is signalled by whether any bytes remain to be read, with no explicit prefix.

attributes.name: item
docs: The type carried by the option when present.
Wraps another type and delimits it with a constant sentinel value written immediately after the wrapped type.

attributes.name: type
docs: The wrapped type whose extent is delimited by the sentinel.

attributes.name: sentinel
docs: The constant value written immediately after the wrapped type to mark its end.
A unique-valued collection. The item type is defined by `item`; the size is determined by the `count` strategy.

attributes.name: item
docs: The type of each item in the set.

attributes.name: count
docs: The strategy used to determine the number of items.
Wraps another type with a numeric prefix indicating the byte length of the wrapped type.

attributes.name: type
docs: The wrapped type whose serialisation is preceded by its size.

attributes.name: prefix
docs: The numeric type used as the size prefix.
A SOL amount expressed in lamports under the wrapped numeric type.

attributes.name: number
docs: The numeric type used to serialise the lamport amount.
A string value.
The encoding describes how its bytes are written.
The byte length is determined by an enclosing wrapper such as `sizePrefixTypeNode` or `fixedSizeTypeNode`.

attributes.name: encoding
docs: The byte encoding used to serialise the string.

attributes.name: display
docs: Display metadata describing how the string is presented.
A named field within a struct type.

attributes.name: name
docs: The name of the field.

attributes.name: defaultValueStrategy
docs: How a configured default value is exposed in generated APIs. Required when `defaultValue` is set.

attributes.name: docs
docs: Markdown documentation for the field.

attributes.name: type
docs: The type of the field.

attributes.name: defaultValue
docs: A default value used when the field is omitted by callers.

attributes.name: display
docs: Display metadata describing how the field is presented.
A composite type made of an ordered list of named fields. Fields are encoded and decoded in declaration order.

attributes.name: fields
docs: The fields of the struct, in declaration order.
A heterogeneous fixed-length sequence in which each positional slot has its own type.

attributes.name: items
docs: The type of each positional slot, in order.
An optional value whose absence is signalled by a designated zero value rather than a presence flag.

attributes.name: item
docs: The type carried by the option when present.

attributes.name: zeroValue
docs: The constant value that signals absence. When omitted, the all-zero byte pattern of the item type is used.

unions.name: standaloneTypeNode
docs: Every type node that can be used as a top-level type.

unions.name: enumVariantTypeNode
docs: The variant flavours of an `enumTypeNode`.

unions.name: typeNode
docs: The composable form: any standalone type, or a reference to a defined type via `definedTypeLinkNode`.

unions.name: registeredTypeNode
docs: Every node tagged as a type-shaped node, including variants and struct fields.

nestedUnions.name: nestedTypeNode
docs: A type, possibly wrapped in zero-or-more size, offset, sentinel, or hidden prefix/suffix modifiers.
The wrapping is recursive: each modifier wraps another `nestedTypeNode<T>` until the inner `T` is reached.

categories.name: value
docs: Value nodes — concrete values whose shape is described by a type node.
A concrete array value: a list of value nodes.

attributes.name: items
docs: The items of the array, in order.
A concrete boolean value.

attributes.name: boolean
docs: The boolean value.
A concrete bytes value, encoded as text in the chosen encoding.

attributes.name: data
docs: The bytes encoded as a text string per the `encoding` attribute.

attributes.name: encoding
docs: The encoding used to represent the bytes as text.
A typed constant: a type node paired with a concrete value node.

attributes.name: type
docs: The type of the constant.

attributes.name: value
docs: The concrete value of the constant.
A concrete value of a defined enum: a variant identifier plus an optional payload.

attributes.name: variant
docs: The name of the selected variant.

attributes.name: enum
docs: A link to the defined enum type the value belongs to.

attributes.name: value
docs: The variant payload — a struct value for struct variants or a tuple value for tuple variants.
Omitted for unit variants.
A value resolved by key from a surrounding provider.
A `providedNode` higher in the resolution tree supplies the actual value; the consumer references only the `key`, so the same type stays portable across instructions that wire the key differently.
Resolution is a per-context property: a value with the same key may resolve in one instruction and not another.

attributes.name: key
docs: The key looked up against the surrounding provide/inject graph.

attributes.name: fallback
docs: A value used when no provider supplies the key.
When absent, the key is required: a provider must supply it for the surrounding context to be valid.
A single (key, value) pair inside a `mapValueNode`.

attributes.name: key
docs: The entry key.

attributes.name: value
docs: The entry value.
A concrete map value: a list of (key, value) entries.

attributes.name: entries
docs: The entries of the map, in order.
The "absent" value for an optional type.
A concrete numeric value.
Stored as a 64-bit float; consumers narrow to a specific integer or float width based on the surrounding type context.

attributes.name: number
docs: The numeric value.
A concrete public key, with an optional symbolic identifier for the address.

attributes.name: publicKey
docs: The base58-encoded public key.

attributes.name: identifier
docs: A symbolic name for the address, useful in generated client code.
A concrete set value: a list of unique value nodes.

attributes.name: items
docs: The items of the set.
The "present" value for an optional type, wrapping a concrete value node.

attributes.name: value
docs: The wrapped value.
A concrete string value.

attributes.name: string
docs: The string value.
A named field of a `structValueNode`.

attributes.name: name
docs: The name of the field.

attributes.name: value
docs: The concrete value of the field.
A concrete struct value: a list of named field values.

attributes.name: fields
docs: The named fields of the struct value.
A concrete tuple value: a fixed-length sequence of positional value nodes.

attributes.name: items
docs: The positional items of the tuple, in order.

unions.name: standaloneValueNode
docs: Every value node that can be used as a top-level value.

unions.name: valueNode
docs: The composable form: any standalone value node.

unions.name: registeredValueNode
docs: Every node tagged as a value-shaped node, including container variants.

unions.name: enumValuePayload
docs: The payload kinds an `enumValueNode` may carry — struct fields or positional tuple slots.

unions.name: injectableNumberValueNode
docs: A concrete number value, or a key resolved at presentation time from a surrounding provider.

unions.name: injectableStringValueNode
docs: A concrete string value, or a key resolved at presentation time from a surrounding provider.

categories.name: link
docs: Link nodes — references to other named entities (programs, PDAs, accounts, …).
A reference to an account defined elsewhere — possibly in a different program.

attributes.name: program
docs: The program the referenced account belongs to. When omitted, the surrounding program is assumed.

attributes.name: name
docs: The name of the referenced account.
A reference to a defined type — possibly in a different program.

attributes.name: program
docs: The program the referenced type is defined in. When omitted, the surrounding program is assumed.

attributes.name: name
docs: The name of the referenced defined type.
A reference to an account of another instruction.

attributes.name: instruction
docs: The instruction the referenced account belongs to. When omitted, the surrounding instruction is assumed.

attributes.name: name
docs: The name of the referenced instruction account.
A reference to an argument of another instruction.

attributes.name: instruction
docs: The instruction the referenced argument belongs to. When omitted, the surrounding instruction is assumed.

attributes.name: name
docs: The name of the referenced instruction argument.
A reference to an instruction defined elsewhere — possibly in a different program.

attributes.name: program
docs: The program the referenced instruction belongs to. When omitted, the surrounding program is assumed.

attributes.name: name
docs: The name of the referenced instruction.
A reference to a PDA defined elsewhere — possibly in a different program.

attributes.name: program
docs: The program the referenced PDA belongs to. When omitted, the surrounding program is assumed.

attributes.name: name
docs: The name of the referenced PDA.
A reference to a program by name.

attributes.name: name
docs: The name of the referenced program.

unions.name: registeredLinkNode
docs: Every node tagged as a link to another part of the IDL.

unions.name: linkNode
docs: The composable form: any registered link node.

categories.name: pdaSeed
docs: PDA-seed nodes — the constants and variables a program uses to derive PDAs.
A PDA seed with a constant value (e.g. a UTF-8 string or a fixed byte sequence).

attributes.name: type
docs: The type of the seed value.

attributes.name: value
docs: The constant value to use as the seed — either a literal value or the program ID placeholder.
A PDA seed whose value is provided at derivation time, identified by name.

attributes.name: name
docs: The name of the seed variable.

attributes.name: docs
docs: Markdown documentation for the seed variable.

attributes.name: type
docs: The expected type of the seed value.

unions.name: registeredPdaSeedNode
docs: Every node tagged as a PDA seed.

unions.name: pdaSeedNode
docs: The composable form: any registered PDA seed node.

unions.name: constantPdaSeedValue
docs: The value forms a `constantPdaSeedNode` may carry — either a literal value or the program ID placeholder.

categories.name: count
docs: Count nodes — strategies for sizing a homogeneous collection in serialized form.
A count strategy that fixes the number of items at a constant value.

attributes.name: value
docs: The fixed number of items.
A count strategy where the number of items is read from a numeric prefix.

attributes.name: prefix
docs: The numeric type used as the count prefix.
A count strategy where items are read until the buffer is exhausted.

unions.name: registeredCountNode
docs: Every node tagged as a count strategy.

unions.name: countNode
docs: The composable form: any registered count node.

categories.name: discriminator
docs: Discriminator nodes — strategies for distinguishing one account or instruction from another.
Identifies a node by a constant value at a known byte offset (e.g. a magic header).

attributes.name: offset
docs: The byte offset at which the constant begins.

attributes.name: constant
docs: The constant value expected at the offset.
Identifies a node by the value of a named field at a known byte offset.

attributes.name: name
docs: The name of the discriminating field.

attributes.name: offset
docs: The byte offset of the field.
Identifies a node by its expected total byte size.

attributes.name: size
docs: The expected byte size.

unions.name: registeredDiscriminatorNode
docs: Every node tagged as a discriminator strategy.

unions.name: discriminatorNode
docs: The composable form: any registered discriminator node.

categories.name: display
docs: Display nodes — presentation metadata attached to instructions, accounts, fields, and enum variants.
Display metadata that presents a number as a scaled amount with an optional unit.
The value is divided by `10 ^ decimals` and rendered alongside `unit` (e.g. `"USDC"`, `"%"`, `"bps"`).

attributes.name: decimals
docs: How many decimal places scale the underlying integer. Resolved as a number value: either a literal `numberValueNode` or a key resolved from a surrounding provider.
A value of `1000000` with `decimals` resolving to `6` renders as `1`.
When this input cannot resolve, renderers should fall back to presenting the raw value rather than guess the scale.

attributes.name: unit
docs: A label appended after the scaled value (e.g. `"USDC"`, `"%"`, `"bps"`). Resolved as a string value: either a literal `stringValueNode` or a key resolved from a surrounding provider.
When this input cannot resolve, renderers should present the scaled value without a unit.
Display metadata that presents a number as a point in time.
The underlying value counts ticks since the Unix epoch; `ticksPerSecond` is the divisor that converts those ticks back to seconds.

attributes.name: ticksPerSecond
docs: How many ticks make one second. Defaults to `1` (the value is already in seconds).
Common choices are `1000` (milliseconds), `1000000` (microseconds), and `1000000000` (nanoseconds).
Display metadata that presents a number as an elapsed duration.
The underlying value counts ticks; `ticksPerSecond` is the divisor that converts those ticks back to seconds.
Renderers typically format the result as `HH:mm:ss` or a coarser human-readable form.

attributes.name: ticksPerSecond
docs: How many ticks make one second. Defaults to `1` (the value is already in seconds).
Common choices are `1000` (milliseconds), `1000000` (microseconds), and `1000000000` (nanoseconds).
Display metadata for an enum variant: its label and whether to hide its inner payload.

attributes.name: label
docs: An override label shown for the variant (e.g. `"Buy"`).
When absent, renderers derive a label from the variant `name`.

attributes.name: skipInnerData
docs: When `true`, the variant's payload is hidden — only the label is rendered.
Useful for tuple payloads that have no per-field handle, or when the payload is purely structural.
Display metadata for an instruction account: its label in the fallback list and whether it is shown.

attributes.name: label
docs: An override label shown in the fallback list (e.g. `"To"`).
When absent, renderers derive a label from the account `name`.

attributes.name: skip
docs: Whether the account is shown in the fallback list. Defaults to `"never"` (always shown).
Display metadata for an instruction: a short intent label and an interpolated sentence template.
Either form may be absent; presentation strategy is left to the renderer.

attributes.name: intent
docs: A short imperative label describing what the instruction does (e.g. `"Transfer"`).

attributes.name: interpolatedIntent
docs: A sentence template that composes the instruction into prose with `${root.path}` placeholders.
Roots are `data.` (an instruction argument) and `accounts.` (an instruction account); the path is flat after the root (e.g. `${data.amount}`, `${accounts.destination}`).
A placeholder renders through its referent's own presentation; the `skip` rule governs the fallback list only and never the sentence.
Display metadata for a string value.
The string's wire encoding is carried by `stringTypeNode.encoding`; this node only addresses presentation.

attributes.name: sliceStart
docs: The start index of the displayed slice, inclusive. Defaults to the start of the string.
Indices apply to the decoded character sequence.

attributes.name: sliceEnd
docs: The end index of the displayed slice, exclusive. Defaults to the end of the string.
Indices apply to the decoded character sequence.
Display metadata for a named member: its label, whether it is shown in the fallback list, and whether it is flattened into its parent.
Value presentation is carried by the member's type; this node only addresses naming and composition.

attributes.name: label
docs: An override label shown for the member (e.g. `"Amount"`).
When absent, renderers derive a label from the member `name`.

attributes.name: skip
docs: Whether the member is shown in the fallback list. Defaults to `"never"` (always shown).

attributes.name: flatten
docs: When `true`, the member's type is expected to be a struct and its fields are lifted into the parent's context, dropping the field name as an extra level of nesting.
Flattening lives on the field rather than on the struct so the same struct can be flattened in one place and nested in another.
Meaningful only when the member's type is structurally a struct; renderers ignore it otherwise.

attributes.name: flattenPrefix
docs: A literal prefix prepended to each flattened member's label (e.g. `"args."`).
Meaningful only when `flatten` is `true`. Useful to disambiguate when two flattened children might collide.

unions.name: numberDisplayNode
docs: The presentation forms a number may take. Raw rendering is expressed by the absence of a display attribute.

unions.name: registeredDisplayNode
docs: Every node tagged as display metadata.

unions.name: displayNode
docs: The composable form: any registered display node.

categories.name: contextualValue
docs: Contextual-value nodes — references resolved at instruction-build time (account values, argument values, …).
Refers to the bump seed of a named PDA-derived account in the surrounding instruction.

attributes.name: name
docs: The name of the account whose bump seed is referenced.
Refers to a field of a named account's decoded data.
The referenced account must carry an `accountLink` so the account's layout is known.
Resolving the value requires reading the account state at presentation time.

attributes.name: account
docs: The name of the referenced account in the surrounding instruction.

attributes.name: path
docs: The name of the field within the account's decoded data.
When absent, the value is the whole decoded account data.
Refers to a named account in the surrounding instruction.

attributes.name: name
docs: The name of the referenced account.
Refers to a named argument of the surrounding instruction.

attributes.name: name
docs: The name of the referenced argument.
A branching contextual value.
The condition resolves to a value at instruction time; that result selects between `ifTrue` and `ifFalse`.

attributes.name: condition
docs: The value whose evaluation drives the branch.

attributes.name: value
docs: When present, the condition result is compared for equality against this value.
Otherwise the result is treated as a boolean.

attributes.name: ifTrue
docs: The value used when the condition resolves truthy (or matches `value`).

attributes.name: ifFalse
docs: The value used when the condition resolves falsy (or does not match `value`).
Refers to the wallet identity providing the instruction context.
Refers to the wallet paying for the surrounding transaction.
Pairs a PDA seed name with the value to substitute when deriving the PDA.

attributes.name: name
docs: The name of the seed being filled in.

attributes.name: value
docs: The value to substitute for the seed.
Resolves to a PDA derived from a list of seed values.

attributes.name: pda
docs: The PDA being derived — either a link to a defined PDA or an inline `pdaNode`.

attributes.name: seeds
docs: The seed values used to derive the PDA, paired with their seed names.

attributes.name: programId
docs: The program ID used to derive the PDA. When omitted, the PDA’s declared program is used.
Refers to the program ID of the surrounding instruction.
A custom resolver: a named function provided by the consumer that produces a value.
May optionally depend on other accounts and arguments resolved at instruction-build time.

attributes.name: name
docs: The name of the resolver function.

attributes.name: docs
docs: Markdown documentation for the resolver.

attributes.name: dependsOn
docs: The accounts and arguments the resolver depends on. Used by clients to ensure the dependencies are resolved first.

unions.name: standaloneContextualValueNode
docs: Every contextual-value node usable as a top-level value.

unions.name: contextualValueNode
docs: The composable form: any standalone contextual-value node.

unions.name: registeredContextualValueNode
docs: Every node tagged as a contextual-value node, including helper variants.

unions.name: instructionInputValueNode
docs: Anything that can be used as the input value for an instruction account or argument default.
Covers concrete values, contextual references, and program links.

unions.name: conditionalValueCondition
docs: The condition forms accepted by a `conditionalValueNode`.

unions.name: resolverDependency
docs: The dependency forms accepted by a `resolverValueNode`.

unions.name: pdaSeedValueValue
docs: The value forms accepted by a `pdaSeedValueNode`.

unions.name: pdaValuePda
docs: A `pdaValueNode` may reference a PDA either by link or inline.

unions.name: pdaValueProgramId
docs: The program-id forms accepted by a `pdaValueNode`.

categories.name: shared
docs: Shared enumerations referenced from multiple node categories.

enumerations.name: bytesEncoding

variants.name: base16
docs: Hexadecimal encoding (two characters per byte).

variants.name: base58
docs: Base58 encoding, the standard for Solana addresses.

variants.name: base64
docs: Base64 encoding (RFC 4648).

variants.name: utf8
docs: UTF-8 text encoding.
How a string of bytes is encoded for transport.

enumerations.name: defaultValueStrategy

variants.name: omitted
docs: The attribute is not exposed as a parameter in the generated API; the default value is always used.

variants.name: optional
docs: The attribute is exposed as an optional parameter; callers may override the default value.
How an attribute that carries a default value is exposed in generated APIs.

enumerations.name: displaySkip

variants.name: always
docs: The member is never shown in the fallback list. Use for purely structural fields like discriminators.

variants.name: never
docs: The member is always shown in the fallback list. This is the default.

variants.name: whenInjected
docs: The member is shown only when its value was not already surfaced elsewhere through the provide/inject graph.
When the value was pulled via injection, the member is hidden as redundant; when nothing surfaced it, the member appears under its label as a backup.
Whether a member should be hidden from the fallback display list.
The interpolated sentence on `instructionDisplayNode` is governed separately — a member may be referenced there regardless of its skip value.

enumerations.name: endianness

variants.name: be
docs: Big-endian: the most significant byte is written first.

variants.name: le
docs: Little-endian: the least significant byte is written first.
The byte order of a numeric serialization.

enumerations.name: instructionLifecycle

variants.name: archived
docs: No longer included in client SDKs. Retained in the IDL for historical reference only.

variants.name: deprecated
docs: Still callable but discouraged. Clients should migrate to a replacement instruction.

variants.name: draft
docs: Work-in-progress. The instruction may change before it stabilises.

variants.name: live
docs: Stable and supported for production use.
The lifecycle stage of an instruction.

enumerations.name: numberFormat

variants.name: f32
docs: IEEE-754 32-bit floating point.

variants.name: f64
docs: IEEE-754 64-bit floating point.

variants.name: i8
docs: Signed 8-bit integer.

variants.name: i16
docs: Signed 16-bit integer.

variants.name: i32
docs: Signed 32-bit integer.

variants.name: i64
docs: Signed 64-bit integer.

variants.name: i128
docs: Signed 128-bit integer.

variants.name: shortU16
docs: Solana compact-u16 encoding: a variable-length unsigned integer occupying 1 to 3 bytes.

variants.name: u8
docs: Unsigned 8-bit integer.

variants.name: u16
docs: Unsigned 16-bit integer.

variants.name: u32
docs: Unsigned 32-bit integer.

variants.name: u64
docs: Unsigned 64-bit integer.

variants.name: u128
docs: Unsigned 128-bit integer.
The wire format of a numeric serialization.

enumerations.name: optionalAccountStrategy

variants.name: omitted
docs: The account slot is left out of the instruction entirely. Subsequent accounts shift up.

variants.name: programId
docs: The account slot is filled with the program ID as a placeholder, preserving positional indices.
How an absent optional account is represented when serialising an instruction.

enumerations.name: postOffsetStrategy

variants.name: absolute
docs: Move the cursor to the absolute byte position given by the offset.

variants.name: padded
docs: Pad with zero bytes from the current cursor up to the offset bytes ahead.

variants.name: preOffset
docs: Restore the cursor to where it was before the wrapped type ran (cancelling its pre-offset).

variants.name: relative
docs: Advance the cursor by the offset bytes relative to its current position.
How a post-offset modifier interprets its offset value after serialising the wrapped type.

enumerations.name: preOffsetStrategy

variants.name: absolute
docs: Move the cursor to the absolute byte position given by the offset.

variants.name: padded
docs: Pad with zero bytes from the current cursor up to the offset bytes ahead.

variants.name: relative
docs: Advance the cursor by the offset bytes relative to its current position.
How a pre-offset modifier interprets its offset value before serialising the wrapped type.

enumerations.name: programOrigin

variants.name: anchor
docs: The program was originally described by an Anchor IDL.

variants.name: shank
docs: The program was originally described by a Shank IDL.
The toolchain that originally generated a program description.

categories.name: topLevel
docs: Top-level nodes and helper unions — the entry points of any Codama IDL.
An on-chain account: its name, data structure, optional fixed size, optional PDA, and optional discriminators.

attributes.name: name
docs: The name of the account.

attributes.name: size
docs: The size of the account in bytes, when the data length is fixed.

attributes.name: docs
docs: Markdown documentation for the account.

attributes.name: data
docs: The struct describing the account data.

attributes.name: pda
docs: A link to the PDA the account is derived from, if applicable.

attributes.name: discriminators
docs: Discriminators that distinguish this account from others in the program.
When multiple are listed, they are combined with a logical AND.
A named constant exposed by the program: a typed value associated with a name.

attributes.name: name
docs: The name of the constant.

attributes.name: docs
docs: Markdown documentation for the constant.

attributes.name: type
docs: The type of the constant.

attributes.name: value
docs: The concrete value of the constant.
A reusable named type that can be referenced by `definedTypeLinkNode` from elsewhere in the IDL.

attributes.name: name
docs: The name of the defined type.

attributes.name: docs
docs: Markdown documentation for the type.

attributes.name: type
docs: The type definition.
A program error — a numeric code paired with a name and human-readable message.

attributes.name: name
docs: The name of the error.

attributes.name: code
docs: The numeric error code returned by the program.

attributes.name: message
docs: A human-readable description of the error.

attributes.name: docs
docs: Markdown documentation for the error.
A program event: its data shape and optional discriminators used to identify it on the wire.

attributes.name: name
docs: The name of the event.

attributes.name: docs
docs: Markdown documentation for the event.

attributes.name: data
docs: The type describing the event payload.

attributes.name: discriminators
docs: Discriminators that distinguish this event from others. When multiple are listed, they are combined with a logical AND.
An account participating in an instruction, with its name, signing/writability flags, and an optional default value.

attributes.name: name
docs: The name of the account.

attributes.name: isWritable
docs: Whether the instruction may write to the account.

attributes.name: isSigner
docs: Whether the account must sign the transaction.
The literal `"either"` indicates a slot that may or may not sign depending on context.

attributes.name: isOptional
docs: Whether the account slot may be omitted by callers.

attributes.name: docs
docs: Markdown documentation for the account slot.

attributes.name: defaultValue
docs: A default value used to fill the slot when the caller does not provide one.

attributes.name: accountLink
docs: A reference to the account's data layout. Required for consumers (e.g. `accountFieldValueNode`) to read fields from the account.
The link's optional `program` allows cross-program references via the root's `additionalPrograms`.

attributes.name: display
docs: Display metadata describing how the account is presented.
A named argument of an instruction, with its type and an optional default value.

attributes.name: name
docs: The name of the argument.

attributes.name: defaultValueStrategy
docs: How a configured default value is exposed in generated APIs. Required when `defaultValue` is set.

attributes.name: docs
docs: Markdown documentation for the argument.

attributes.name: type
docs: The type of the argument.

attributes.name: defaultValue
docs: A default value used when the argument is omitted by callers.

attributes.name: display
docs: Display metadata describing how the argument is presented.
A byte-size delta applied when computing rent or buffer size — typically used by instructions that resize accounts.

attributes.name: withHeader
docs: Whether the delta includes the account header overhead.

attributes.name: subtract
docs: When `true`, the delta is subtracted from the running size instead of added. Defaults to `false`.

attributes.name: value
docs: The source of the delta value — a literal number, a referenced account or argument, or a resolver.
A program instruction: its accounts, arguments, byte-delta hints, discriminators, optional status, and optional sub-instructions.

attributes.name: name
docs: The name of the instruction.

attributes.name: docs
docs: Markdown documentation for the instruction.

attributes.name: optionalAccountStrategy
docs: How absent optional accounts are represented when serialising the instruction.

attributes.name: accounts
docs: The accounts the instruction operates on, in order.

attributes.name: arguments
docs: The serialised arguments of the instruction, in order.

attributes.name: extraArguments
docs: Additional arguments exposed in the generated client API but not serialised on the wire.

attributes.name: remainingAccounts
docs: Variable-length tails of accounts appended after the named account slots.

attributes.name: byteDeltas
docs: Byte-size adjustments applied when computing rent or buffer size — for instructions that resize accounts.

attributes.name: discriminators
docs: Discriminators that distinguish this instruction from others.
When multiple are listed, they are combined with a logical AND.

attributes.name: status
docs: The lifecycle status of the instruction.

attributes.name: subInstructions
docs: Inner instructions invoked through CPI as part of executing this instruction.

attributes.name: provides
docs: Named nodes exposed to consumers in the surrounding scope.
Each entry pairs with an `injectedValueNode` that references it by key, so reusable types can pull contextual values without naming siblings directly.

attributes.name: display
docs: Display metadata describing how the instruction is presented.
A "remaining accounts" slot in an instruction — a variable-length tail of accounts derived from a value.

attributes.name: isOptional
docs: Whether the remaining-accounts tail may be empty.

attributes.name: isSigner
docs: Whether each remaining account must sign the transaction.
The literal `"either"` indicates a slot that may or may not sign depending on context.

attributes.name: isWritable
docs: Whether the instruction may write to each remaining account.

attributes.name: docs
docs: Markdown documentation for the remaining-accounts slot.

attributes.name: value
docs: The source of the remaining-accounts list — a referenced argument or a resolver.

attributes.name: display
docs: Display metadata describing how the remaining-accounts group is presented as a whole.
The lifecycle stage of an instruction (draft, live, deprecated, archived) with an optional accompanying message.

attributes.name: lifecycle
docs: The lifecycle stage.

attributes.name: message
docs: Free-form prose accompanying the status — e.g. a deprecation notice with migration guidance.
A program-derived address: its name, optional program ID override, and the seeds used to derive it.

attributes.name: name
docs: The name of the PDA.

attributes.name: docs
docs: Markdown documentation for the PDA.

attributes.name: programId
docs: The base58-encoded program ID used to derive the PDA. When omitted, the surrounding program is assumed.

attributes.name: seeds
docs: The seeds used to derive the PDA, in order.
A Solana program: its identity, version, accounts, instructions, defined types, PDAs, events, errors, and constants.

attributes.name: name
docs: The name of the program.

attributes.name: publicKey
docs: The base58-encoded program ID.

attributes.name: version
docs: The version of the program, in semver form.

attributes.name: origin
docs: The toolchain that originally generated the program description, if known.

attributes.name: docs
docs: Markdown documentation for the program.

attributes.name: accounts
docs: The accounts owned by the program.

attributes.name: instructions
docs: The instructions exposed by the program.

attributes.name: definedTypes
docs: The reusable types defined by the program.

attributes.name: pdas
docs: The PDAs derived by the program.

attributes.name: events
docs: The events emitted by the program.

attributes.name: errors
docs: The errors returned by the program.

attributes.name: constants
docs: The constants exposed by the program.
Exposes a node under a name so consumers in the surrounding scope can resolve it by that key.
Sits inside a host's `provides` list and pairs with `injectedValueNode` on the consumer side: an injection with the matching key resolves to this entry's `node`.

attributes.name: name
docs: The key under which the node is exposed to consumers.

attributes.name: node
docs: The exposed node. The provider is a transparent pipe — any node may be supplied; the family check happens at the injection point against the consumer's expected family.
The root of a Codama IDL document.
Pairs a primary program with any number of additional programs and tags the document with the spec version.

attributes.name: standard
docs: A literal marker identifying the document as a Codama IDL.

attributes.name: version
docs: The Codama spec version this document conforms to.

attributes.name: program
docs: The primary program described by the document.

attributes.name: additionalPrograms
docs: Additional programs referenced by the primary program.

unions.name: instructionByteDeltaValue
docs: The value forms accepted by an `instructionByteDeltaNode`.

unions.name: instructionRemainingAccountsValue
docs: The value forms accepted by an `instructionRemainingAccountsNode`.
